content.text:
<user_query>
after updating all the monorepo dependencies to keep things in order I got this issue:
> org@0.1.0 build /Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org
> vite build && tsc

vite v7.3.2 building client environment for production...
✓ 41 modules transformed.
✗ Build failed in 71ms
error during build:
[vite]: Rollup failed to resolve import "tiny-warning" from "/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org/node_modules/@tanstack/react-router/dist/esm/Mat
ches.js".
This is most likely unintended because it can break your application at runtime.
If you do want to externalize this module explicitly add it to
`build.rollupOptions.external`
    at viteLog (file:///Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/config.js:33639:57)
    at file:///Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/config.js:33673:73
    at onwarn (file:///Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/@vitejs/plugin-react/dist/index.js:76:7)
    at file:///Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/config.js:33673:28
    at onRollupLog (file:///Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/config.js:33668:63)
    at onLog (file:///Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/config.js:33471:4)
    at file:///Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/rollup/dist/es/shared/node-entry.js:21369:32
    at Object.logger [as onLog] (file:///Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/rollup/dist/es/shared/node-entry.js:23364:9)
    at ModuleLoader.handleInvalidResolvedId (file:///Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/rollup/dist/es/shared/node-entry.js:22108:26)
    at file:///Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/rollup/dist/es/shared/node-entry.js:22066:26
 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.
</user_query>

Let me investigate the dependency situation.

The issue is that `@tanstack/react-router` is trying to import `tiny-warning` but it's not installed. This is likely a peer dependency issue after updating dependencies. Let me investigate the current state of things.

input.description: Check org package.json for relevant deps

input.command: cd /Users/duhl/git/ui && ls apps/org/node_modules/@tanstack/react-router/dist/esm/Matches.js 2>/dev/null && head -30 apps/org/node_modules/@tanstack/react-router/dist/esm/Matches.js 2>/dev/null || echo "File not found"
cd /Users/duhl/git/ui && ls apps/org/node_modules/tiny-warning 2>/dev/null || ls node_modules/tiny-warning 2>/dev/null || echo "tiny-warning not found"

content.text:
The issue is clear: `@tanstack/react-router` v1.157.16 imports `tiny-warning` but it's not installed. Let me check if it's supposed to be a dependency of the router package or if it needs to be added explicitly.

So `@tanstack/react-router` v1.157.16 is installed and it imports `tiny-warning`, but `tiny-warning` isn't installed anywhere. Let me check if it's listed as a dependency of `@tanstack/react-router`.

`tiny-warning` is listed as a dependency of `@tanstack/react-router` but pnpm isn't installing it. This is likely a pnpm hoisting/resolution issue. Let me check.

`tiny-warning` is listed as a dependency of `@tanstack/react-router`, but it's not being installed. This is likely a pnpm hoisting issue. Let me check if there's a `.npmrc` or pnpm configuration that might be affecting this.

input.command: cd /Users/duhl/git/ui && pnpm why tiny-warning 2>/dev/null || echo "not found"

content.text:
`tiny-warning` isn't installed at all despite being a dependency of `@tanstack/react-router`. This can happen with pnpm when the lockfile gets out of sync during a bulk dependency update. The simplest fix is to reinstall:

`pnpm why tiny-warning` returned nothing, meaning it's not installed at all. This is strange since `@tanstack/react-router` lists it as a direct dependency. With `node-linker=hoisted`, pnpm should be hoisting packages. Let me try reinstalling or checking if there's a lockfile mismatch.

Let me try a simpler fix first - just install `tiny-warning` in the org app.

input.command: cd /Users/duhl/git/ui && ls node_modules/tiny-warning/package.json 2>/dev/null && echo "Found" || echo "Still missing"

content.text:
Still missing. Let me dig deeper into the lockfile to understand why it's not being resolved.

Still missing after `pnpm install`. This is strange. Let me check the lockfile for `tiny-warning` and also check if there are other versions of `@tanstack/react-router` that might not need it.

Actually, looking more carefully - the `package.json` in the org app lists `"@tanstack/react-router": "^1.131.5"` but the installed version is `1.157.16`. This is a significant version jump. The issue might be that the lockfile resolved to a version that needs `tiny-warning` but the lockfile itself doesn't include `tiny-warning` properly.


Let me examine the lockfile to see what's actually recorded there.

input.description: Check if tiny-invariant is in lockfile

content.text:
`tiny-warning` is completely absent from the lockfile, but `tiny-invariant` is there. This means the lockfile was generated without `tiny-warning` being resolved. This is likely because the version of `@tanstack/react-router` in the lockfile was resolved at a time when it didn't need `tiny-warning`, but then the actually installed version (from the tarball or resolved version) does need it.

Let me check the lockfile for the `@tanstack/react-router` entry.

I see. The lockfile has resolved `@tanstack/react-router` to version `1.168.22` but the node_modules has version `1.157.16`. There's a discrepancy. Let me check this more carefully.

Wait, actually the `package.json` in the org app specifies `"@tanstack/react-router": "^1.131.5"` and the installed version in `apps/org/node_modules/@tanstack/react-router` is `1.157.16`. But the lockfile shows `1.168.22`. This suggests the node_modules are out of date.


With `node-linker=hoisted`, there could be a version conflict where the org app is getting a different version than what the lockfile expects. I need to check if there's a `@tanstack/react-router` in the root node_modules as well to understand the actual resolution.

input.command: cd /Users/duhl/git/ui && node -e "const pkg = require('./node_modules/@tanstack/react-router/package.json'); console.log('Root version:', pkg.version); console.log('Dependencies:', JSON.stringify(pkg.dependencies, null, 2))"

cd /Users/duhl/git/ui && node -e "const pkg = require('./apps/org/node_modules/@tanstack/react-router/package.json'); console.log('Org version:', pkg.version); console.log('Dependencies:', JSON.stringify(pkg.dependencies, null, 2))"

content.text:
Found the issue. The root `node_modules` has `@tanstack/react-router@1.168.22` (which dropped the `tiny-warning` dependency), but there's a stale nested copy at `apps/org/node_modules/@tanstack/react-router@1.157.16` that still needs `tiny-warning`. Let me clean this up.

Now the picture is clear:

- The **root** `node_modules` has `@tanstack/react-router@1.168.22`, which does NOT depend on `tiny-warning` (it was removed in a newer version).
- The **org app** has a nested copy at `apps/org/node_modules/@tanstack/react-router@1.157.16`, which DOES depend on `tiny-warning`.

With `node-linker=hoisted`, pnpm resolved the lockfile for the newer version (1.168.22) which doesn't need `tiny-warning`, so `tiny-warning` was never added to the lockfile. 

The org app's nested 1.157.16 is stale from a previous install, and since the lockfile was resolved to 1.168.22, `tiny-warning` never got included. I need to either clean and reinstall, or update the org app's dependency to match what's in the lockfile.

input.description: Check what's nested in org node_modules

content.text:
These are stale nested copies from April 9 (before your dependency update). The lockfile was updated but pnpm didn't clean them out. Let me nuke the stale nested `node_modules` and reinstall properly.

These nested copies are from April 9, before the dependency update. The lockfile was updated but these stale nested copies weren't cleaned up. Let me remove them and reinstall.

input.command: cd /Users/duhl/git/ui && ls apps/org/node_modules/@tanstack/react-router/package.json 2>/dev/null && echo "Still has nested copy" || echo "No nested copy - using hoisted"

content.text: Good, the stale nested copy is gone. Now let me verify the build works.

The Vite build passes now (the `tiny-warning` issue is resolved). The remaining failures are all TypeScript type errors, which are a separate issue from the missing dependency. These fall into two categories:

1. **Zod + TanStack Form `StandardSchemaV1` type incompatibilities** - `ZodCoercedNumber`/`ZodCoercedDate` types now expose `unknown` as their input type, which conflicts with TanStack Form's stricter type checking. This is a known breaking change when updating `@tanstack/react-form` and `zod` together.

2. **Vitest test matcher types** - `toBeVisible` and `toHaveClass` matchers aren't recognized (likely needs `@testing-library/jest-dom` type augmentation for Vitest).

3. **Minor type issues** - `'{}'` not assignable to `'string'` in two index files, and a readonly index signature issue.

Would you like me to dig into and fix the TypeScript errors as well, or was the `tiny-warning` build resolution what you were after?
